---
title: "Before You Begin: Setup"
---

::: {.callout-note}
### Session at a Glance

**Total core time: about TODO minutes.**

| Section | Time | Type |
|---|---|---|
| Install the tools | TODO | Hands-on |
| Restore the course library | TODO | Hands-on |
| Check you are ready | TODO | Hands-on |

TODO: fill in the time budget once the session is written.
:::

## What You Will Install

TODO: R, an IDE (RStudio or Positron), Quarto, and Git.

## Why This Course Pins Its Packages

TODO: explain that reproducibility is part of the subject matter in
regulated clinical work, and that this repository practises what it
teaches: `renv.lock` records the exact version of every package the
course uses, and restoring it is your first taste of a reproducible
pipeline. The lockfile is a teaching artifact, not overhead.

## Restore the Course Library

TODO: walk through cloning the repo and running, once, in R at the
project root:

```r
renv::restore()
```

TODO: show what a successful restore looks like, and note the expected
duration (minutes, not tens of minutes — the project's `.Rprofile`
points Linux machines at precompiled binaries; macOS and Windows get
binaries by default).

## Check You Are Ready

TODO: a short self-test snippet the learner runs to confirm the
environment works, with the expected output shown.

## If Something Went Wrong

TODO: the most common failure modes, each with its one-line fix
(Windows: install Rtools44; all platforms: `renv::status()` first).
